steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence refers to ‘the Harbor Park bicycle path,’ a specific, already-identified noun. The clause ‘which is about 0.38 miles long’ adds extra information about it — it does not restrict which path is meant. Non-restrictive relative clauses must be enclosed in commas on both sides when they appear mid-sentence.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — path, which is about 0.38 miles long: Places a comma before the clause but not after it, leaving the clause open-ended and unclosed before the main verb. ✗
  • Choice B — path which is about 0.38 miles long: No commas at all. Without commas, the clause reads as restrictive, implying there are multiple Harbor Park bicycle paths and this clause identifies which one — that is not the intended meaning. ✗
  • Choice C — path which is about 0.38 miles long,: Places a comma after the clause but not before it, again violating the rule that both sides of a mid-sentence non-restrictive clause must be set off. ✗
  • Choice D — path, which is about 0.38 miles long,: Commas on both sides correctly enclose the non-restrictive clause, satisfying Standard English conventions. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
